free_text: Investigation of activation cross section data of
alpha particle induced nuclear reaction on molybdenum
up to 40 MeV: Review of production routes of medically
relevant 97,103Ru
Natural high purity metallic Mo foils (12.0 um)
Two stacks containing Mo and Cu monitor foils were
irradiated with alpha beam of 40 MeV and 118 nA and
79 nA for 52 min and 70 min respectively.
Activity of the foils was measured in 3 series to
follow the decay of the reaction products.
No chemical separation was applied.
Energy degradation was calculated by using the semi
empirical formula of Andersen and Ziegler.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of stopping power of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam was
+- 0.3MeV which increased up to +-1.0MeV in the last
target foil of the stack.
The total uncertainty was estimated from the
following independently contributing parameters.
Uncertainty of time parameters was not included.
Overall uncertainty were about 11-15%.
surface density of target nuclei(5%),
incident charged particle flux (7%),
detector efficiency (5%)
statistical and peak fitting (3-10%)
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
data from series 1
data from series 2
